title: cuscuta europaea subsp. viciae
intro: Cuscuta europaea subsp. viciae, also known as dodder, is a parasitic plant that grows by attaching itself to other plants and obtaining nutrients through its host. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Cuscuta europaea subsp. viciae include its ability to improve circulation, boost the immune system, and reduce the risk of certain diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. In herbal preparations, Cuscuta europaea subsp. viciae is often dried and made into capsules or tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
